To the humbly devout woman of Samaria who expected the Messiah, Jesus quietly disclosed, “I that speak unto thee am he” (John 4:26). Yet an anxious Pilate “saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer” (John 19:9). (Neal A. Maxwell, in Conference Report, Apr. 1989, 78; or Ensign, May 1989, 64)

“…an anxious Pilate ‘saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.’ (John 19:9.) “Can we remain silent when silence is eloquence—but may be used against us?
